rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ROBARE VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 25, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Specially appearing defendant National Cooperative Refinery Association¿s motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiffs failed to satisfy their burden of demonstrating that National Cooperative Refinery Association had the requisite contacts with California in order to subject it to personal jurisdiction. Plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance to conduct jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiffs made no showing that their proposed discovery might (much less, would) produce evidence of any contacts of National Cooperative Refinery Association with California. (See Beckman v. Thompson (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 481, 486-487.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
